title: A note for you in six months
checkLabels.t3: The heading must still be there
checkLabels.t5: The sentence must still be there
checkLabels.t4: The image must still be there
why.empty: The editor is empty. You deleted the page instead of leaving yourself a note in it. Bring the code back.
why.not-closed: A tag is open and never closed, or closed in the wrong order.
why.no-comment: There is no comment. The syntax is odd and hard to remember: <!-- in front, --> behind.
why.not-closed-comment: You opened the comment but never closed it. --> is missing. An unclosed comment eats everything after it — the whole page disappears.
why.lost-content: Something is gone from the page. You are adding a comment, not deleting. And careful: you may have commented it out — and commented code does not exist.
why.lost-img: The image is gone. You may have commented it out. Commented code does not exist — to the browser it is not there.
hint2.no-comment: The syntax is <!-- text -->. See the example.
hint3.no-comment: Put it somewhere in <body>, before the image for instance.
hint2.not-closed-comment: --> is missing. Look at where your comment ends.
hint3.not-closed-comment: Without the closing --> the browser thinks the comment runs to the end of the file. And eats everything.
hint2.lost-content: Check that nothing ended up INSIDE the comment. Between <!-- and --> nothing exists.
hint2.lost-img: Check the image did not end up INSIDE the comment. Between <!-- and --> nothing exists.
blocks.0.text: Your code is getting long. Five or six lines doing different things. Today it is all clear to you.
blocks.1.text: In six months you will open it and think: "what is this, who wrote it, why is it like that". You wrote it. You just forgot.
blocks.4.text: The syntax is odd and hard to remember. <!-- in front, --> behind.
blocks.5.text: Put one comment in your code — anywhere. Run. Nothing appears on the page.
blocks.7.text: Not the browser. Not Google. Not the blind person. Only someone who opens the code — and that someone is usually you.
blocks.8.text: Careful: commented code DOES NOT EXIST. Wrap something in <!-- --> and to the browser it is gone. That is handy when you want to switch something off for a moment. And it is a trap when you forget.
blocks.9.text: "You in six months open the file and cannot understand what you wrote."
